citations.description: det_floor.py imports: logging, re, dataclass, field, Any — no _OPERATOR_ATTESTED_TAG_RE
_OPERATOR_ATTESTED_TAG_RE is defined here, not in det_floor
workflow_ops imports from det_operator_attested, not det_floor
unit test imports _OPERATOR_ATTESTED_TAG_RE from det_operator_attested, docstring contradicts AC

findings.criterion: The lint and the completion-verifier agree on 'tagged': det_floor owns _OPERATOR_ATTESTED_TAG_RE and workflow_ops imports it. Proof: `python -c "from rebar.llm.plan_review.det_floor import _OPERATOR_ATTESTED_TAG_RE as a; from rebar.llm.plan_review.workflow_ops import _OPERATOR_ATTESTED_AC_RE as b; assert a is b"`
findings.detail: The implementation placed _OPERATOR_ATTESTED_TAG_RE in src/rebar/llm/plan_review/det_operator_attested.py (a new module extracted for module-size reasons), NOT in det_floor.py. det_floor.py's imports are logging, re, dataclass, field, Any — it does not define or re-export _OPERATOR_ATTESTED_TAG_RE. The stated proof command `from rebar.llm.plan_review.det_floor import _OPERATOR_ATTESTED_TAG_RE as a` would raise ImportError. workflow_ops does import `_OPERATOR_ATTESTED_TAG_RE as _OPERATOR_ATTESTED_AC_RE`, but from det_operator_attested, not det_floor. The unit test test_tag_regex_single_source (line 150-152) also imports from det_operator_attested and its docstring explicitly says 'det_operator_attested OWNS the [operator-attested] matcher' — contradicting the AC's 'det_floor owns' requirement. The single-source intent is satisfied but the named owner (det_floor) is not the actual owner.
findings.remediation: Either re-export _OPERATOR_ATTESTED_TAG_RE from det_floor.py (e.g., `from .det_operator_attested import _OPERATOR_ATTESTED_TAG_RE`) so the proof command works, or update the AC to name det_operator_attested as the owner and adjust the proof command accordingly.

citations.description: Ticket status is 'in_progress'; the only comment is the E1 descope note with no Gerrit change number or merge commit sha.

findings.criterion: [operator-attested] The change is landed on main through Gerrit (LLM-Review +1 AND Verified +1, no unresolved comments), attested on this ticket with the Gerrit change number(s) and the merge commit sha.
findings.detail: The ticket is still in_progress and has only one comment (the E1 descope note by Joe Oakhart, 2026-07-16). No Gerrit change number, no merge commit sha, and no attestation of landing through the two-vote gate is recorded anywhere in the ticket comments. This operator-attested criterion requires a concrete attestation naming a Gerrit change id and merge commit sha.
findings.remediation: Record the Gerrit change number(s) (e.g., Change-Id or numeric id at rebar.solutions.navateam.com) and the resulting merge commit sha on this ticket as a comment, confirming LLM-Review +1, Verified +1, and no unresolved comments at the time of merge.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
